title: Wash Yuh Hand, Every Day!
description: A fun calypso song from Grenada about the importance of washing your hands.
lyrics:
[Chorus]
Wash yuh hand, every day! [CLAP, CLAP]
Keep de germs far away! [SNAP, SNAP]
With soap and water, make it clean,
The cleanest hands you've ever seen!

[Verse 1]
After playing in de sand,
Wash yuh hand, understand?
Before you eat a mango sweet,
Wash yuh hand, from head to feet!

[Chorus]
Wash yuh hand, every day! [CLAP, CLAP]
Keep de germs far away! [SNAP, SNAP]
With soap and water, make it clean,
The cleanest hands you've ever seen!

[Verse 2]
If you cough, or if you sneeze,
Wash yuh hand, if you please!
Germs dem spread like wildfire,
So washing hands, is what we require!

[Chorus]
Wash yuh hand, every day! [CLAP, CLAP]
Keep de germs far away! [SNAP, SNAP]
With soap and water, make it clean,
The cleanest hands you've ever seen!

[Verse 3]
'Fore you touch yuh face, my friend,
Wash yuh hand, 'til the very end!
"Tek care" (Take care) is what we say,
Healthy and strong, every day!

[Chorus]
Wash yuh hand, every day! [CLAP, CLAP]
Keep de germs far away! [SNAP, SNAP]
With soap and water, make it clean,
The cleanest hands you've ever seen!
thumbnailPrompt: A vibrant and colorful illustration for a children's song about handwashing. Depict happy Grenadian children dancing on a sunny beach with palm trees in the background. One child is demonstrating how to wash their hands at an outdoor sink. Include musical notes floating in the air and a steelpan drum in the foreground. The children are wearing colorful Caribbean clothing. The overall style should be cheerful and cartoonish.
educationalValue: Teaches children the importance of handwashing to prevent the spread of germs and stay healthy. Introduces the Grenadian phrase "Tek care" (Take care).
